---
title: " "
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    df_print: paged
    css: styles.css
---

<div class="page-title">Curriculum Vitae</div>

<div class="cv-actions">
<a class="cv-download" href="Lonnie_Ernst_Resume.pdf" download>Download one-page resume (PDF)</a>
</div>

## Professional Summary

Biotechnologist with a B.S. in Biotechnology and laboratory experience spanning academic and GMP manufacturing settings across molecular biology, nucleic acid extraction and quantification, PCR, and microbiology. My background combines hands-on research at an academic biomanufacturing lab with reagent formulation in a GMP production environment, giving me a foot in both the discovery and production sides of biotech. I'm especially drawn to work at the intersection of molecular biology and environmental application. My current research explores engineering cyanobacteria to break down persistent organic pollutants while suppressing toxin production. Alongside the bench, I'm increasingly focused on bringing data analysis and computational tools into laboratory work.

<div class="chevron-divider"><span></span><span></span><span></span></div>

## Professional Experience

<div class="cv-timeline">

<div class="timeline-entry">
<div class="timeline-role">Specialist III, Media Production Coordinator</div>
<div class="cv-meta">Salt Lake Community College InnovaBio · Sep 2021 – Present</div>

- Perform high-complexity molecular biology procedures including PCR, nucleic acid extraction and quantification, agarose gel electrophoresis, restriction digest, NanoDrop quantification, and pH measurement.
- Participate in the development, optimization, and validation of new assays and protocols; write and maintain SOPs and troubleshoot assays.
- Train laboratory personnel in molecular techniques, contamination control, equipment operation, data recording, experimental troubleshooting, and results analysis; deliver annual pGLO transformation instruction to junior high students.
- Lead media production for biology departments producing 1,000+ orders per semester across 15+ media types including LB, nutrient agar, MacConkey, PDA, SIM, Simmons citrate, and gelatin stab with full QC including viability and sterility controls.
- Manage inventory, equipment purchases, vendor communications, departmental order coordination, and routine equipment maintenance; maintain cost-tracking systems including production cost analysis and billing.

**Key Projects & Techniques:**

*BPH Gene Cluster (PCB Degradation Research):*  Primer design, PCR optimization, Gibson Assembly preparation, plasmid transformations, midiprep, restriction digest, plasmid construction design, SOP development, and protocol research.

*Taq Polymerase Production (StudentFactured):*  IPTG induction and protein expression, cell density quantification (DeNovix DS-11 FX+), SDS-PAGE, and column-free protein purification via ammonium sulfate precipitation and dialysis.

*PETase-MHETase (Yeast Engineering):*  Yeast culturing, plasmid transformation of YPH499, synthetic media production, and fluorescence verification under UV.

*Cyanobacterial O₂ Production (Mars Conditions):*  Cyanobacterial culturing in a photobioreactor, BG-11 growth medium production, and operation of O₂/CO₂ sensors and anaerobic chamber.

</div>

<div class="timeline-entry">
<div class="timeline-role">Formulator II</div>
<div class="cv-meta">bioMérieux · May 2023 – Present</div>

- Perform aseptic formulation of buffers, reagents, and PCR components for molecular diagnostic platforms in a high-throughput GMP manufacturing environment processing up to 36,000 pouches per day requiring precision pipetting at scale (~12,000 pipette operations daily).
- Formulate and dispense magnetic beads using an automated dispenser; operate and troubleshoot freeze dryers including error diagnosis and escalation protocols.
- Maintain rigorous contamination control through daily equipment cleaning, weekly deep cleans, and consistent aseptic practices in compliance with GMP standards.
- Complete batch records accurately and verify protocol compliance; manage bi-weekly inventory and reagent reordering to maintain production continuity.
- Train Form 1 personnel in all lab practices, GMP documentation, reagent preparation, and contamination-control expectations.

</div>

<div class="timeline-entry">
<div class="timeline-role">Microbiologist</div>
<div class="cv-meta">Analytical Research Labs · May 2022 – May 2023</div>

- Performed high-throughput aseptic sample preparation and microbial enumeration for nutraceutical, dietary supplement, and food manufacturers processing 100+ samples per day including serial dilutions, TSA and PDA plating at multiple dilution factors, coliform pad dispensing, and broth tube preparation.
- Produced in-house media including TSA and PDA plates and dilution media using a MediaClave and automated plate pourer; performed daily equipment validation (scales and pH meter).
- Prepared positive plate controls using live pathogens; maintained strict aseptic and sterile technique throughout sample handling and disposed of biohazardous waste in compliance with safety protocols.
- Tracked and documented samples through full chain of custody using LIMS software; verified sample logging at end of shift.
- Served as night shift team lead for sample preparation, directing a small team to meet testing deadlines under time pressure in a high-throughput environment.

</div>
</div>

## Education

### Bachelor of Science, Biotechnology
<div class="cv-meta">Utah Valley University · May 2026</div>

Relevant coursework: Molecular Biology, Genetics, Genomics, Cell Biology, Microbiology, Organic Chemistry, Biochemistry I, Bioinformatics, Data Analysis, Phylogenetics.

<div class="chevron-divider"><span></span><span></span><span></span></div>

## Technical Skills

<div class="skills-grid">
<div class="skills-col">
<div class="skill-group">
### Molecular Biology & Microbiology
PCR, Nucleic Acid Extraction & Quantification, Spectrophotometry (NanoDrop, OD600), Agarose Gel Electrophoresis, SDS-PAGE, Restriction Digest, Miniprep & Midiprep, Plasmid Design & Construction, Serial Dilution, Aseptic & Sterile Technique, Microbial Culture & Enumeration, Protein Expression (IPTG Induction), Protein Purification (Ammonium Sulfate Precipitation & Dialysis), BSL-2 Operations, Contamination Control, Biohazardous Waste Handling
</div>
<div class="skill-group">
### Genomics & Diagnostics
NGS Principles, DNA Extraction from Biological Samples, Genotyping Concepts, Sequence Analysis, BLAST, Phylogenetic Analysis
</div>
<div class="skill-group">
### Data & Quality
SOP Writing & Execution, Assay Development & Validation, Batch Record Completion, Quality Control, LIMS, Data Review, GMP Compliance, Daily Equipment Validation, Inventory Management
</div>
</div>
<div class="skills-col">
<div class="skill-group">
### Biomanufacturing & Production
GMP Manufacturing, Reagent & Buffer Formulation, PCR Master Mix Formulation, Magnetic Bead Dispensing (Automated), Media Production (TSA, PDA, LB, MacConkey, SIM, Simmons Citrate, Gelatin Stab, Broth Tubes), Freeze Drying & Lyophilization, High-Throughput Pipetting, Plate Pouring (Automated)
</div>
<div class="skill-group">
### Instruments & Equipment
NanoDrop & DeNovix DS-11 FX+, MediaClave, Automated Dilution Equipment, Automated Plate Pourer, BioTek MultiFlo Automated Dispenser, Freeze Dryers, Photobioreactor, Anaerobic Chamber, O2 & CO2 Sensors, pH Probe, Autoclave, Peristaltic Pump, Thermocycler, UV Transilluminator
</div>
<div class="skill-group">
### Computing
Data Analysis & Visualization (R), Command Line & Bash Scripting, Electronic Lab Notebook, MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint, Google Workspace, NCBI Datasets, Benchling
</div>
</div>
</div>

## Research

### Presentations

**Evolution of Microcystins Across Cyanobacteria**
Poster presentation. UVU Department of Biology, Utah Valley University, Orem, UT. April 2026.
[View poster (PDF)](images/poster-phylo-2026.pdf)

**Modifying Cyanobacteria to Break Down Polychlorinated Biphenyls**
Poster presentation. Undergraduate Projects, Performances, Presentations & Research Celebration (UP3RC), Salt Lake Community College, Salt Lake City, UT. May 2024.
[View poster (PDF)](images/poster-bph-up3rc-2024.pdf)

**Sustainability of Cyanobacteria in a CO2 Rich Environment**
Poster presentation. Utah NASA Space Grant Consortium, Utah State University, Logan, UT. April 2024.
[View poster (PDF)](images/poster-mars-2024.pdf)

**Modifying Cyanobacteria to Break Down Polychlorinated Biphenyls**
Poster presentation. Utah Conference on Undergraduate Research (UCUR), Utah Valley University, Orem, UT. February 2024.
[View poster (PDF)](images/poster-bph-ucur-2024.pdf)

<div class="chevron-divider"><span></span><span></span><span></span></div>

For a detailed overview of current and past research projects, see the [Research page](research.html).
